$schema_note: G37 latency budgets for the hot endpoints. p95_ms is the wall-clock 95th-percentile ceiling asserted by tests/test_perf_smoke.py (in-process TestClient) and referenced by perf/k6_load_test.js thresholds. Budgets are deliberately generous relative to observed local p95 (single-digit ms on the deterministic/mock paths) so the CI smoke is a catastrophic-regression guard, not a tight benchmark that flakes on a slow shared runner. Measure locally with perf/README.md before tightening.

GET /api/workspaces.note: Workspace list; deterministic DB read. Observed local p95 ~2-3ms.

GET /api/workspaces/{id}.note: Workspace overview aggregate. Observed local p95 ~4-5ms.

POST /api/workspaces/{id}/qa.note: Cited extractive Q&A (BM25 retrieval, mock LLM, no network). Observed local p95 ~3-4ms.

GET /api/workspaces/{id}/search.note: Full-text artifact search (SQLite tokenized-LIKE). Observed local p95 ~6-7ms.

POST /api/workspaces/{id}/underwriting/c.note: LBO underwriting compute (CPU-bound, no persistence). Observed local p95 ~4-5ms.
